methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $8.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Reflection AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $8B

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $3.3B

data.narrative:
Alpha School is a Series A–stage AI & Software company founded by MacKenzie Price and Brian Holtz. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—including Reflection AI ($8.0B Series A), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B Series B), Together AI ($3.3B Series A), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B Series B)—supplemented by a broader universe of 8 private and 1 public comparable (Palantir, ticker PLTR).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable valuation technique given data availability. The median valuation across the 4-company peer set is $4.5B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $8.0B. This method captures current market pricing for AI & Software firms at proximate development stages and reflects recent capital market activity in the sector.

The triangulated valuation range is $3.8B (low) to $5.1B (high), centered on a median of $4.5B. Key uncertainties include the limited sample size of directly comparable Series A peers and stage variance within the peer set—Physical Intelligence and Black Forest Labs are Series B companies, which typically command premiums. The wide dispersion within the peer range ($3.2B to $8.0B) reflects material heterogeneity in these businesses, making stage-adjusted comparables and company-specific risk factors essential to any final valuation judgment.
